title: Bridge Essay: [Cognitive explanations of auditory verbal hallucinations in schizophrenia: An inventory of the scientific literature].
concept: Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

**[Cognitive explanations of auditory verbal hallucinations in schizophrenia: An inventory of the scientific literature].** — Encephale (2020). DOI: 10.1016/j.encep.2019.11.010. PMID: 32156419. AIMS: Our article consists in both analysis and synthesis of contemporary cognitive models of auditory verbal hallucinations (AVHs) in schizophrenia. Our work is based on the analysis of the scientific literature including original articles, literature reviews as well as meta-analysis. METHODOLOGY: In order to identify the most pertinent studies in the electronic search, the three following databases were systematically searched: PubMed, PsycINFO and MEDLINE. For both the analysis and synthesis

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 13 (Daimon). Evaluation: Excellent (score=4.8)
